steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Predict Principal Action Resource Condition · Theory Principal Actio…
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 6700 during a browser upload compatibility rollout. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to evaluate a runtime-validated explicit-deny-aware tenant authorization record; do not treat TypeScript types, a resolved promise, CORS, ETag, or an emulator as stronger proof than it is; then change region, retrieval, or transfer cost and defend the revised budget action. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, evaluate identity and resource policies by principal, action, exact resource, condition, session, explicit deny, and organization boundary.
